title: Heat
short: Energy transferred between bodies because of a temperature difference, measured in joules (J).
full:
Heat is energy in transit: it flows from a hotter body to a colder one purely because of their temperature difference. It is not a substance stored in an object — what an object stores is internal (thermal) energy; heat is the transfer.

When heat flows into an object without changing its phase, its temperature rises by an amount set by its mass and specific heat capacity: Q = c·m·ΔT.

The SI unit is the joule (J). The calorie (1 cal ≈ 4.186 J) is an older unit still seen in nutrition.
